steps.action: Navigate to /benchmarks/9b9d3b22-9571-4591-b6dd-4f4f199e17f3
steps.expected: BenchmarkPage loads showing completed state with comparison view
steps.observed: BenchmarkPage loaded with 'Benchmark Results', 'Comparing 2 URLs', 'completed' status pill, and full comparison view displayed

steps.action: Verify comparison view is showing (completed state)
steps.expected: Trust Score Comparison section visible with URL cards
steps.observed: Trust Score Comparison section visible with example.com/ (100%) and example.org/ (100%) cards with ProgressMeter components and View Report links

steps.action: Verify polling mechanism exists in code
steps.expected: 1.5s polling pattern in useEffect when status is running/queued
steps.observed: BenchmarkPage.tsx line ~93: `timer = window.setTimeout(fetchBenchmark, 1500)` — polls every 1.5s while status is 'running' or 'queued'. Stops polling when completed/failed. Per-URL progress section (data-testid='progress-section') renders ProgressMeter per URL during running state.

steps.action: Verify per-URL progress rendering in code
steps.expected: Progress indicators per URL during running state
steps.observed: Code renders benchmark-progress-list with benchmark-progress-item per URL showing URL name and progress percentage with ProgressMeter. Aggregate progress shown in hero section.

steps.action: Check BenchmarkPage.test.tsx for polling and progress tests
steps.expected: Tests confirm polling behavior
steps.observed: Tests 'polls every 1.5s while benchmark status is running', 'stops polling when completed', 'shows per-URL progress while benchmark is running', 'shows aggregate progress meter while running' all present and verify correct behavior

assertions.title: BenchmarkPage shows side-by-side trust score comparison

steps.action: Verify Trust Score Comparison section visible
steps.expected: Section with heading 'Trust Score Comparison' displayed
steps.observed: Section with h2 'Trust Score Comparison' and subtitle 'URLs ranked by trust score, highest first. Delta shows the spread between best and worst.' visible

steps.action: Verify URLs ranked by trust score highest first
steps.expected: URLs displayed in descending trust score order
steps.observed: Both URLs at 100% so ordering is valid. DOM snapshot confirms: 'example.com/ 100%' then 'example.org/ 100%'. Code sorts with `.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)` confirming highest-first ordering.

steps.action: Verify ProgressMeter components visible
steps.expected: Visual progress bars under each score
steps.observed: ProgressMeter gradient bars visible under each 100% score in screenshot. Code renders `<ProgressMeter value={score || 0} />` for each non-failed URL.

steps.action: Verify delta indicator between best and worst scores
steps.expected: Delta badge showing point spread (0 for identical scores)
steps.observed: No delta badge shown because both scores are 100% (delta=0). Code only renders delta-badge when `scoreDelta > 0`. Test 'calculates correct delta indicator between best and worst scores' verifies delta=60 for (90,60,30) spread. This is correct behavior.

steps.action: Verify Scenario Breakdown section visible
steps.expected: Grid with heading 'Scenario Breakdown'
steps.observed: Section with h2 'Scenario Breakdown' and subtitle 'Finding counts per scenario across all URLs. Rows are scenarios, columns are URLs.' visible

steps.action: Verify grid structure: rows=scenarios, columns=URLs
steps.expected: Header row with Scenario + URL columns, data rows with scenario names
steps.observed: Grid header: 'Scenario | example.com/ | example.org/'. Two data rows: 'Cookie Consent | 0 | 0' and 'Subscription Cancellation | 0 | 0'

steps.action: Verify cells with zero findings show '0' not blank
steps.expected: All zero-finding cells display '0'
steps.observed: All 4 cells display '0' (not blank). Code uses `scenarioGridData[scenario]?.[url] ?? 0` which defaults to 0. Screenshot confirms '0' visible in all cells.

steps.action: Verify selected scenarios are shown
steps.expected: cookie_consent and subscription_cancellation rows present
steps.observed: Both selected scenarios rendered as 'Cookie Consent' and 'Subscription Cancellation' (titleized). Only selected scenarios shown — no N/A rows for unselected scenarios.

steps.action: Verify Unified Summary section visible
steps.expected: Section with heading 'Unified Summary'
steps.observed: Section with h2 'Unified Summary' and subtitle 'Key insights across all audited URLs.' visible

steps.action: Verify (a) URL with highest trust score named
steps.expected: Highest scoring URL identified
steps.observed: Card labeled 'HIGHEST SCORING' shows 'example.com/' with '100% trust score'

steps.action: Verify (b) URL with lowest trust score named
steps.expected: Lowest scoring URL identified
steps.observed: Card labeled 'LOWEST SCORING' shows 'example.org/' with '100% trust score'

steps.action: Verify (c) dark pattern family mentioned if findings exist
steps.expected: Pattern families listed or 'No patterns detected' if none
steps.observed: Card labeled 'COMMON PATTERNS' shows 'No patterns detected' — correct since both audits have 0 findings. Code renders pattern-badge elements when topPatterns.length > 0.

steps.action: Verify (d) overall risk word displayed
steps.expected: One of: low, moderate, high, critical
steps.observed: Card labeled 'OVERALL RISK' shows 'Low' with 'Average trust score: 100%'. Risk word logic: avgScore >= 70 = 'low', 50-69 = 'moderate', 30-49 = 'high', < 30 = 'critical'.

evidence.network: Data derived from previously fetched audit and findings responses

assertions.title: Navigation from BenchmarkPage to individual audit reports

steps.action: Verify View Report links exist for each URL
steps.expected: View Report link per URL pointing to /audits/{id}/report
steps.observed: Two 'View Report' links found: (1) href='/audits/94843e6d-5bfc-4ba5-a58b-3988450889ee/report?benchmark=9b9d3b22-9571-4591-b6dd-4f4f199e17f3' (example.com), (2) href='/audits/05251856-283b-462c-a8b1-795db5adf293/report?benchmark=9b9d3b22-9571-4591-b6dd-4f4f199e17f3' (example.org)

steps.action: Click View Report for example.com audit
steps.expected: Navigate to /audits/94843e6d-5bfc-4ba5-a58b-3988450889ee/report
steps.observed: Successfully navigated to http://localhost:5173/audits/94843e6d-5bfc-4ba5-a58b-3988450889ee/report?benchmark=9b9d3b22-9571-4591-b6dd-4f4f199e17f3. ReportPage rendered correctly showing Trust Audit Report with 100/100 score.

steps.action: Verify back link or breadcrumb on ReportPage returns to /benchmarks/{benchmarkId}
steps.expected: A 'Back to Benchmark' link pointing to /benchmarks/9b9d3b22-9571-4591-b6dd-4f4f199e17f3
steps.observed: NO 'Back to Benchmark' link found. ReportPage has: 'Open HTML report', 'Download PDF', 'Compare Personas', 'Back to run log', 'Back to History' — but no benchmark back link. ReportPage.tsx contains zero references to 'benchmark'. The ?benchmark= query param is passed in the URL but not consumed by ReportPage.

steps.action: Verify browser back button returns to BenchmarkPage
steps.expected: Browser back navigates to BenchmarkPage
steps.observed: Browser back button successfully returned to /benchmarks/9b9d3b22-9571-4591-b6dd-4f4f199e17f3. However, this is browser history, not an explicit back link.

evidence.network: Navigation: BenchmarkPage -> ReportPage -> BenchmarkPage (via browser back)

assertions.issues: ReportPage does not implement a 'Back to Benchmark' link or breadcrumb. The BenchmarkPage correctly passes ?benchmark={id} in the View Report link, but ReportPage.tsx does not read this query parameter and does not render a back-to-benchmark navigation element. The assertion requires 'A back link or breadcrumb on ReportPage returns to /benchmarks/{benchmarkId}' which is missing.

steps.action: Check if error-badge CSS class exists in styles
steps.expected: .error-badge class defined in CSS
steps.observed: .error-badge class defined at index.css:1675 with display:inline-flex, padding, border-radius, background, font styling

steps.action: Check if trust-score-na CSS class exists in styles
steps.expected: .trust-score-na class defined for N/A display
steps.observed: .trust-score-na class defined at index.css:1705 with font-family Space Grotesk, 36px, bold

steps.action: Check BenchmarkPage.tsx code for partial failure handling
steps.expected: Code conditionally renders error badge and N/A for failed audits
steps.observed:
Line ~328: `const isFailed = score === null && auditData?.status === 'failed'`. Line ~331: card gets 'failed' class when isFailed. Line ~336: `{isFailed && Error}`. Lines ~339-340: renders `
N/A
` when isFailed, otherwise renders score with ProgressMeter.

steps.action: Check sortedUrls handles null scores (failed audits at end)
steps.expected: Failed audits sorted to end of list
steps.observed: Sort logic: null scores sorted after numeric scores with `if (a.score === null) return 1; if (b.score === null) return -1;`

steps.action: Check BenchmarkPage.test.tsx for partial failure test
steps.expected: Test exists verifying error badge and N/A rendering
steps.observed: Test 'shows error badge and N/A score for failed audits' at line 604 creates benchmark with trust_scores={failed-site: null, success-site: 75}, mock audit with status 'failed', and asserts: (1) error badge visible in comparison section, (2) N/A text visible for failed audit

steps.action: Verify benchmark status field has valid transitions
steps.expected: Valid statuses: queued, running, completed, failed
steps.observed: BenchmarkStatus type in types.ts: 'queued' | 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed'. Current benchmark status='completed' confirmed via API. BenchmarkPage renders status pill with class `status-${benchmark.status}`.

steps.action: Check current DOM for error-badge (should not exist since no failures)
steps.expected: No error-badge in DOM for all-successful benchmark
steps.observed: document.querySelector('.error-badge') returned null — correct since both audits succeeded

evidence.network: GET /api/benchmarks/{id} -> 200 with status='completed' and trust_scores={example.com: 100.0, example.org: 100.0}

frictions.description: Both benchmark audits have 0 findings and empty scenario_breakdown arrays, making the scenario grid show all zeros and the summary show 'No patterns detected'. This limits visual verification of the grid and pattern family display, but code review confirms correct handling.
frictions.resolution: Verified behavior via code review and unit test coverage in BenchmarkPage.test.tsx which uses mock data with non-zero findings

frictions.description: Both trust scores are identical (100.0), so delta badge is not visible (only shown when scoreDelta > 0). Cannot visually verify ranking since all scores are equal.
frictions.resolution: Code review confirms correct sort logic (highest first) and delta calculation. Unit test 'calculates correct delta indicator between best and worst scores' verifies delta=60 for (90,60,30) data.

frictions.description: Cannot trigger partial audit failure in mock mode — all audits complete successfully. VAL-BENCH-012 verified through code and test review only.
frictions.resolution: Verified via BenchmarkPage.tsx source code (error-badge rendering, N/A logic, sort handling) and BenchmarkPage.test.tsx unit test 'shows error badge and N/A score for failed audits'

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 5 passed, 1 failed. VAL-BENCH-011 failed because ReportPage does not implement a 'Back to Benchmark' link — the ?benchmark= query param is passed but not consumed by ReportPage.tsx. All other BenchmarkPage features work correctly: comparison view renders with trust scores and ProgressMeter (VAL-BENCH-008), scenario breakdown grid shows proper data with '0' not blank (VAL-BENCH-009), unified summary shows all required elements (VAL-BENCH-010), polling/progress mechanism exists in code (VAL-BENCH-007), and partial failure handling is properly implemented with error-badge and N/A rendering (VAL-BENCH-012).
